text:
This is a sample text file for testing chunking functionality.

Paragraph one contains information about data conversion.
It demonstrates how text can be split into logical chunks.

Paragraph two shows another section. This helps test the paragraph-based chunking mode.

Paragraph three is the final section. Each paragraph is separated by blank lines.
This makes it easy to identify boundaries when chunking.

Sentence-based chunking works differently. It splits text by sentences. Each sentence ends with punctuation. This is useful for natural language processing.

Character-based chunking divides text into fixed-size pieces. Word-based chunking groups by word count. Line-based chunking splits by newlines.

All chunking modes support overlaps. This means adjacent chunks can share some content. Overlapping is useful for maintaining context across chunk boundaries.
